content.text: Why JSX Whitespace Is Surprising

JSX does not treat whitespace the same way HTML does or the same way plain JavaScript strings do. The Babel/SWC JSX transform applies specific whitespace-trimming rules when compiling JSX text nodes. Understanding these rules prevents confusing rendering gaps (or lack thereof) and explains why sometimes you need explicit techniques to preserve intentional spaces.

attrs.alt: Diagram showing JSX whitespace trimming rules on the left and techniques for preserving intentional whitespace on the right

content.text: The Four Whitespace Rules
The JSX compiler applies these rules to text content inside elements:
Lines that contain only whitespace are removed entirely.
A blank line between JSX elements produces no text node.
Leading whitespace on a line is removed.
Indentation at the start of a text line is stripped.
Trailing whitespace on a line is removed.
Spaces at the end of a text line before the newline are stripped.
Newlines are condensed to a single space — or removed.
A newline adjacent to another element boundary is removed; a newline within a text run becomes one space.
These rules make multi-line JSX readable without introducing unwanted whitespace into the DOM — but they also mean whitespace you might expect to be there is silently dropped.
Whitespace Between Inline Elements
A common confusion: putting two inline elements on separate lines results in no space between them in the rendered output:
The
pattern injects a string expression containing a single space. Because it is an expression (not bare text), the whitespace rules do not apply — the space is passed through as-is.
Blank Lines
Blank lines between JSX elements produce no output:
JSX blank lines are meaningless for output. Use CSS for spacing between elements, not JSX formatting.
Preserving Intentional Whitespace
Explicit String Expression
The most common technique for preserving a space between inline content is the string expression
Same-Line Placement
Keeping text and inline elements on the same line preserves any spaces that are in the source:
HTML Entities
HTML character references work inside JSX text — they are passed through to the DOM:
Template Literals
JavaScript template literals let you build strings with precise whitespace control before passing them to JSX:
Text in JSX Attributes
Whitespace in JSX attribute values follows different rules depending on how you write them:
Pre and Code Blocks — Verbatim Content
The
<pre>
element preserves whitespace natively in the DOM (
white-space: pre
in the browser's default stylesheet). Use it for code samples, ASCII art, or any content where whitespace is semantic:
dangerouslySetInnerHTML for Verbatim HTML
When you have a string of HTML you want to insert verbatim — preserving its whitespace exactly — use
dangerouslySetInnerHTML
. This bypasses JSX parsing entirely:
Warning:
Only use
dangerouslySetInnerHTML
with sanitized HTML. Unsanitized user input injected as innerHTML enables XSS attacks. Use a library like DOMPurify to sanitize before rendering.
The children Prop and Text
When children include text, React normalizes whitespace the same way the JSX compiler does. Reading
props.children
gives you the post-normalization text:
Common Whitespace Bugs and Fixes
Symptom
Cause
Fix
Space between two words is missing
Inline elements on separate JSX lines
Use {' '} or put on same line
Extra space appears unexpectedly
Trailing space on a text line before closing tag
Usually harmless; trim the source
Blank line adds visible gap
Blank line between elements (does not happen — blank lines are removed)
Use CSS margin/gap for spacing
Code snippet loses indentation
JSX collapses whitespace in text nodes

Use 
{code}
or CSS white-space: pre
'Name:Alice' stuck together
Newline between and text trimmed
{' '} after the element or same-line placement
Whitespace in Lists
Rendering lists from arrays produces no implicit separators. React places array elements adjacent to each other with no whitespace between them:
Summary

JSX applies four whitespace-trimming rules during compilation: blank lines and leading/trailing line whitespace are removed; internal runs of spaces collapse to one; newlines at element boundaries are stripped. These rules keep JSX source readable without polluting the DOM with indentation whitespace. To preserve intentional spaces: use
between elements, keep text on the same line as adjacent inline elements, use HTML entities for non-breaking or typographic characters, and use
<pre>
for verbatim whitespace-sensitive content. Use CSS (
gap
margin
) for visual spacing between elements rather than blank lines or space characters.
Rendering Numbers and Booleans
JSX renders JavaScript numbers as their string representation. Booleans render as nothing. This affects how you compose dynamic text:
A practical consequence: never render an object directly as a child. If you try, React throws an error:
"Objects are not valid as a React child"
. Always serialize or destructure to a primitive before rendering.
Text Interpolation Patterns
Composing readable text from dynamic values requires care with whitespace. Here are the reliable patterns:
Handling User-Entered Text
JSX escapes text content by default, preventing XSS. When you render user-provided strings in JSX, React automatically escapes HTML special characters:
The automatic escaping of
{text}
is one of React's core security features. Text rendered via
is always safe. Only
dangerouslySetInnerHTML
bypasses this protection.
Localization and Special Characters
When building internationalized apps, text content often comes from translation systems that may include HTML-like markup or special Unicode characters. Key patterns:
Text Measurement and Whitespace

If you need to measure text for layout (e.g. auto-sizing a text input or a canvas label), remember that JSX whitespace normalization affects the actual text content in the DOM. Always measure from the rendered DOM element, not from the raw JSX source:
The hidden span uses
white-space: pre
to measure the text's actual rendered width including spaces and indentation.
Key takeaways:

JSX strips leading/trailing whitespace and blank lines from text nodes. Newlines between inline elements produce no space — use {' '} or same-line placement to insert one. HTML entities work in JSX text. 
{code}
preserves all whitespace. dangerouslySetInnerHTML bypasses JSX parsing entirely (sanitize first). Use CSS gap/margin for element spacing, not whitespace characters.
